No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus connects the course content to poverty eradication or economic resources for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus connects the course content to food security, nutrition, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course aims to create 'safe, comfortable, environmentally friendly and low-environmental load' living spaces, directly contributing to well-being and reducing pollution (Target 3.9).
evidence: 營造安全、舒適、環保以及低環境負荷的居住空間
create a safe, comfortable, environmentally friendly and low-environmental load environment

Quality Education.reason: The course explicitly aims to equip students with professional knowledge for 'pursuing sustainable development,' directly aligning with Target 4.7 on education for sustainable development.
evidence: achieve the goal of pursuing sustainable development
達成追求永續發展之目標

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus connects the course content to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: While green buildings may implicitly involve water efficiency, the syllabus lacks explicit mention of water quality, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course's focus on 'low-environmental load' green buildings strongly implies a focus on energy efficiency, aligning with Target 7.3.
evidence: 低環境負荷的居住空間

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: 'Industry Exchange' activities suggest engagement with the green building sector, promoting technological upgrading and resource efficiency (Targets 8.2, 8.4).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's core focus on 'Green Building,' 'smart technology,' and 'sustainable infrastructure' directly aligns with upgrading infrastructure and promoting clean technologies (Targets 9.1, 9.4).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus connects the course content to reducing inequalities based on income, age, sex, or other status.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course title 'Green Building Smart and Eco-Community' directly and fundamentally addresses sustainable human settlements, urbanization, and community planning (Targets 11.1, 11.3, 11.6).
evidence: 綠色建築與智慧生態社區

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course explicitly aims to 'reduce waste emissions' and discusses 'material use,' directly supporting Target 12.5 (waste reduction) and Target 12.2 (resource efficiency).

Climate Action.reason: The course objectives are explicitly framed 'under climate change,' focusing on architectural design and smart technology for mitigation and adaptation (Targets 13.1, 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus connects the course content to marine ecosystems, ocean pollution, or aquatic life.

Life on Land.reason: The course's focus on 'Eco-Community' and enabling 'people, buildings and the environment to coexist and prosper' aligns with integrating ecosystem values (Target 15.9).
evidence: 人、建築與環境生態能共生共榮

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the syllabus connects the course content to peace, justice, or institutional strength.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: 'Industry Exchange' and 'Cross-Domain Integration Course' activities promote collaboration and multi-stakeholder partnerships, aligning with Target 17.17.
